channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Military Conducts Strikes on Iranian Radar Facilities Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: According to the available article information, the United States carried out strikes targeting radar installations in Iran. The report, published by Agence France-Presse and carried by ABS-CBN News o...

item.title: Nish Kumar discusses comedy, free speech, and responsibility towards marginalised groups
item.description: In an in-depth interview ahead of his new stand-up tour, British comedian Nish Kumar reflects on his career, his approach to comedy, and the increasingly polarised debates surrounding free speech and ...

item.title: US and Iran exchange strikes and warnings amid escalating tensions near the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States and Iran have escalated military tensions in the Gulf region following a series of drone incidents and retaliatory strikes near the strategic Strait of Hormuz. According to US Centra...

item.title: John Carney's 'Power Ballad' with Paul Rudd and Nick Jonas Celebrates Authentic Music Creation in Film
item.description: John Carney, the acclaimed writer-director known for films like 'Once' and 'Sing Street,' returns with 'Power Ballad,' a music-focused comedy-drama starring Paul Rudd as an Ireland-based wedding singe...

item.title: Recurring Air Leaks in ISS Russian Zvezda Module Prompt Temporary Precautionary Shelter in Crew Dragon
item.description: The International Space Station (ISS) experienced a brief precautionary measure on June 5, 2026, when NASA astronauts temporarily sheltered in the Crew Dragon Freedom spacecraft while Russian cosmonau...

item.title: Several Hollywood Celebrities Decline Invitations to Trump's Planned UFC Birthday Event at the White House
item.description: A report published by Raw Story, citing Vanity Fair and other media outlets, says that several well-known Hollywood figures are not expected to attend a UFC event planned at the White House to celebra...

item.title: Marina Hyde questions Nigel Farage’s consistency in responding to high-profile murders and national tragedies
item.description: In this opinion article, Marina Hyde argues that Nigel Farage presents himself as a national voice only when doing so aligns with his political interests. She compares his reactions to three murders t...

item.title: Bipartisan concerns emerge over Trump’s appointment of Bill Pulte as acting intelligence chief
item.description: President Donald Trump’s appointment of Bill Pulte as acting director of national intelligence has sparked significant concern across the political spectrum. According to reporting cited in the articl...

item.title: Trump Ends NBC Interview Early Due to Rain Noise and Disagreement on Election Questions in Wisconsin Barn
item.description: During a visit to Wisconsin on June 5, 2026, President Donald Trump participated in an interview with NBC News' Meet the Press team inside a barn at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, at the White House'...

item.title: Cuba allows Cuban investors at home and abroad to manage hotels amid foreign chain withdrawals
item.description: The Cuban government has announced a new policy allowing Cubans living in Cuba and abroad to manage and invest in the country’s hotel sector, following the withdrawal or reduction of operations by sev...

item.title: Pakistan Strongly Rejects India's Baseless Allegations Regarding Gilgit-Baltistan Polls
item.description: In a firm diplomatic response, Pakistan has categorically rejected India's recent remarks concerning the upcoming elections in Gilgit-Baltistan, terming them as outlandish and part of New Delhi's ongo...

item.title: ECC approves over Rs40bn supplementary grants, expands budget-related honoraria and backs Rs100bn PSO financing facility
item.description: The Economic Coordination Committee (ECC), chaired by Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb, approved more than Rs40 billion in supplementary grants and authorised a Rs100 billion sovereign-guarantee-ba...

item.title: UN Warns Mercury Use in Artisanal Gold Mining Threatens Women's Health and Children's Development in Tanzania
item.description: The Executive Secretary of the Minamata Convention on Mercury, Monika Stankiewicz, has raised concerns about the serious health impacts of mercury use in artisanal and small-scale gold mining, particu...

item.title: Alex Eala dominates Mananchaya Sawangkaew to advance to Birmingham Open semifinals
item.description: Filipina tennis star Alex Eala continued her strong run at the Birmingham Open in the United Kingdom after defeating Thailand’s Mananchaya Sawangkaew in straight sets, 6-3, 6-2, to secure a place in t...

item.title: UK government rebukes JD Vance over comments linking Henry Nowak's murder to immigration
item.description: Downing Street has criticised attempts to use the murder of 18-year-old Henry Nowak to deepen political divisions after US Vice-President JD Vance linked the killing to immigration in a post on X. Van...

item.title: 4.9-magnitude earthquake hits Lahore and nearby regions; no casualties reported
item.description: A 4.9-magnitude earthquake struck Lahore and adjoining areas on Friday, causing panic among residents who rushed out of homes and buildings for safety. According to the National Seismic Monitoring Cen...

item.title: Tanzania Reports Major Progress in Reducing Antibiotic Misuse and Antimicrobial Resistance Deaths
item.description: Tanzania has announced significant progress in its efforts to combat antimicrobial resistance (AMR), reporting an 88 percent reduction in antibiotic consumption and a 14 percent decline in deaths link...

item.title: State-Level Political Battles Set to Shape Nigeria’s 2027 General Election

item.title: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh Faces Pressure to Balance White House Rate-Cut Demands Against Strong Economic Data

item.title: South Africa and Kenya Deepen Ties as Africa Faces Security, Health and Humanitarian Challenges
